import sys

def main():
    text = sys.argv[1]
    n = int(sys.argv[2])
    lines = text.split('\n')
    last_n_lines = '\n'.join(lines[-n:]) + '\n'
    print(last_n_lines)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

@(grammar sigil)
@(stat tests:173 examples:4 stdlib:20)
@(impl ocaml tree-walking-interpreter)
@(bnf
  prog          ::= module | script-form+
  module        ::= ‘(module name imp* fn* test-spec* meta-note?)’
  imp           ::= ‘(import name)’
  fn            ::= ‘(fn name param* -> ty stmt*)’
  lambda        ::= ‘(\' (name | ‘(name+)') stmt*)’
  param         ::= name ty
  stmt          ::= set | call | ret | while | loop | for | foreach
               | if | ifelse | try | cond | break | continue
  set           ::= ‘(set name [ty] expr)’
  call          ::= ‘(ident expr*)’
  ret           ::= ‘(expr)’
  while         ::= ‘(expr stmt+)’
  loop          ::= ‘(expr stmt+)’
  for           ::= ‘(name int int stmt+)’
  foreach       ::= ‘(ident type array stmt+)’
  if            ::= ‘(expr stmt* else?)’
  ifelse        ::= ‘(expr stmt*)’
  try           ::= ‘(body (catch var string handler+))’
  cond          ::= ‘(case+ default?)’
  case          ::= ‘(expr stmt+)’
  break         ::= ‘()’
  continue      ::= ‘()’
  else          ::= ‘(stmt*)’
  body          ::= ‘(stmt*)’
  catch         ::= ‘(var string handler+)’
  expr          ::= ident | int | float | bool | string | array | map | json | regex | process | socket | channel | input | expect | case
               | add | sub | mul | div | mod | eq | neq | lt | gt | le | ge | and | or | not | bit_and | bit_or | bit_xor | bit_not
  ident         ::= ‘[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*’
  int           ::= ‘-?[0-9]+’
  float         ::= ‘-?[0-9]*\.[0-9]+([eE]-?[0-9]+)?’
  bool          ::= ‘true | false’
  string        ::= “[^"]*”
  array         ::= ‘[expr,…]’
  map           ::= ‘{keyval,…}’
  keyval        ::= expr : expr
  json          ::= ‘{...}’
  regex         ::= “/[^/]+/\"
  process       ::= '<process>'
  socket        ::= '<socket>'
  channel       ::= '<channel>'
  input         ::= '<input>'
  expect        ::= '<expect>'
  case          ::= '<case>'
  ty            ::= ‘int | float | bool | string | array | map | json | regex | process | socket | channel | input | expect | case’
  meta-note     ::= ‘(note_type string)’
  test-spec     ::= ‘(case+)’
  script-form   ::= stmt | expr | module)
@(literals
    (array "[1,2,3]")
    (map "{\"key\":\"value\"}")
    (decimal "19.99d"))
@(special-forms
    (and "short-circuit: ‘(and e1 e2) - returns false if e1 is false, e2 not evaluated")
    (or "short-circuit: ‘(or e1 e2) - returns true if e1 is true, e2 not evaluated")
    (cond "'(cond (e1 s1…)(e2 s2…)(true s3…)) - flat multi-branch conditional")
    (try-catch "'(try body (catch var string handler+)) - catches RuntimeError, message bound as string"))
@(ex
    (fn add a int b int  →int ‘(ret (add a b)))
    (fn fact n int  →int
      '(if (eq n 0) (ret 1))
      (ret (mul n (fact (sub n 1)))))
    (fn sum_for arr array  →int
      (set s int 0)
      (set len int (array_length arr))
      '(for i 0 len
        (set s (add s (array_get arr i))))
      (ret s))
    (fn sum_foreach arr array  →int
      (set s int 0)
      '(for-each val int arr
        (set s (add s val)))
      (ret s))
    (set nums array [1,2,3,4,5])
    (set config map {"name":"Alice","age":30})
    '(cond
      ((lt x 0) (set result string "negative"))
      ((eq x 0) (set result string "zero"))
      (true (set result string "positive"))))
@(mod
    (stdlib-modules "20 modules in pure Sigil")
    (core "string_utils conversion array_utils math math_extended filesystem network text_utils validation number_theory char_utils encoding")
    (data "json_utils")
    (net "http")
    (pattern "regex")
    (sys "process")
    (db "sqlite")
    (crypto "base64 hash hmac")
    (paths "stdlib/core/ stdlib/data/ stdlib/net/ stdlib/sys/ stdlib/pattern/ stdlib/db/ stdlib/crypto/")
    (import-syntax "'(import module_name) - auto-searches stdlib subdirs"))
@(note
    (no-comments "Sigil has NO comment syntax  - no ; // # or any other form")
    (explicit-types "type optional everywhere: ‘(set x 0) infers from value. Only required when inference is ambiguous (e.g. empty array_new).")
    (script-mode "files without ‘(module ...)’ wrapper treated as script: top-level ‘(fn ...)’ defs + expressions form implicit main")
    (argv-shorthand "$N expands to ‘(arg_str N), #N to ‘(arg_int N). Save 5+ tokens per CLI arg access.")
    (variadic-println "'(println a b c) prints “a b c\n” with space separator. Single-arg usage unchanged.")
    (fmt-interpolation "'(fmt “hello {name} age {}” age) - named {} looks up in scope, empty {} takes positional args")
    (implicit-main-ret "main returns 0 by default - drop trailing ‘(ret 0)’ boilerplate")
    (no-implicit-conv "use cast_int_float etc - no auto-coercion")
    (type-dispatch "write ‘(add x y) - interpreter infers add_int|add_float|add_decimal from x type")
    (module-keyword "use ‘(module ...)’ NOT ‘(mod ...)’ - mod is modulo")
    (string-slice-params "CRITICAL: string_slice(str start LENGTH) - 3rd arg is LENGTH not end index")
    (array-new-no-args "'(array_new) takes NO parameters - use [1,2,3] for literals")
    (reserved-var-names "CANNOT use as variable names: int float decimal string bool unit array map json regex process socket channel input expect case")
    (reserved-module-names "CANNOT name modules: json array map string int float bool etc")
    (entry-point "fn main -> int required for programs  - test files with test-spec don't need main")
    (if-else "'(if cond then…(else else…)) - else block optional, must be last")
    (cond-construct "'(cond (cond1 body1…)(cond2 body2…)(true default…)) - flat multi-branch, replaces nested if-else")
    (for-loop "'(for var start end body+) - counting loop, i in [start, end), exclusive upper bound, int only")
    (for-each "'(for-each var type collection body+) - iterates array elements or map keys")
    (try-catch "'(try body (catch var string handler+)) - catch type must be string")
    (no-closures "functions do NOT capture outer scope variables  - pass all data as parameters")
    (test-framework "test-spec with case/input/expect - all test files MUST use this")
    (and-or-special "and/or are AST-level short-circuit forms, NOT function calls")
    (decimal-type "arbitrary precision: 0.1 +0.2 =0.3 exactly - use for money")
    (decimal-suffix "19.99d creates decimal literal directly")
    (prefer-string-format "use ‘(string_format “id:{} name:{}” id name)’ over string_concat chains for multi-part strings")
    (print-polymorphic "'(print x) works for int float bool string decimal array map")
    (run "./.interpreter/_build/default/vm.exe program.sigil")
    (build "cd interpreter && eval $(opam env) && dune build"))

python_local.code:
import sys

def filter_filenames(filenames, extension):
    return '\n'.join([filename for filename in filenames.split('\n') if filename.endswith('.' + extension)]) + '\n'

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_data = sys.argv[1]
    target_extension = sys.argv[2]
    result = filter_filenames(input_data, target_extension)
    print(result)

python_local.code:
import sys

def filter_csv_rows(csv_string, column_index, threshold):
    rows = csv_string.strip().split('\n')
    filtered_rows = [row for row in rows if int(row.split(',')[int(column_index)]) > int(threshold)]
    return '\n'.join(filtered_rows)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    csv_input = sys.argv[1]
    column_index = sys.argv[2]
    threshold = sys.argv[3]
    result = filter_csv_rows(csv_input, column_index, threshold)
    print(result)

seen = set()

python_local.code:
import sys

def filter_lines(lines, pattern):
    return [line for line in lines if pattern in line and 'grep' not in line]

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_data = sys.argv[1]
    pattern = sys.argv[2]
    lines = input_data.split('\n')
    filtered_lines = filter_lines(lines, pattern)
    print('\n'.join(filtered_lines))

python_local.code:
import sys

input_data = sys.argv[1]
lines = input_data.split('\n')
output = ''

for line in lines:
    if line:
        parts = line.split(' ', 2)
        if len(parts) > 2:
            author = parts[1]
            message = ' '.join(parts[2:])
            output += f'{author}: {message}\n'

print(output.strip())

python_local.code:
import sys

text = sys.argv[1]
lines = text.split('\n')
result = []

i = 0
while i < len(lines):
    count = 1
    while i + 1 < len(lines) and lines[i] == lines[i + 1]:
        i += 1
        count += 1
    result.append(f'{count:4} {lines[i]}')
    i += 1

print('\n'.join(result))

python_local.code:
import sys

source_code = sys.argv[1]
lines = source_code.split('\n')
function_names = []

for line in lines:
    if line.strip().startswith('def ') and '(' in line:
        function_name = line.split('(')[0].split(' ')[1]
        function_names.append(function_name)

print('\n'.join(function_names))
